mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates healthy demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5 percent annually, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent indicates a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or the threshold effect disappears when mortgage rates fall below 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside a stable or declining federal funds rate creates a favorable supply-demand and financing environment, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when FEDFUNDS rises above 2 percent, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Annualized real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent during early-cycle expansion phases confirms sustained economic momentum and earnings growth, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant after controlling for monetary policy shifts.
